title: NOC 72101 – Tool and die makers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72101 professionals are the master architects of the manufacturing world. As a TEER 2 occupation, Tool and Die Makers design, build, and repair specialized custom-made dies, cutting tools, jigs, and fixtures used in the mass production of metal and plastic parts. In 2026, as Canada ramps up domestic production for the Electric Vehicle (EV) and Aerospace sectors, these high-precision craftsmen are among the most sought-after technical professionals in the country. For immigration, this role is a "Golden Ticket" for Express Entry Category-Based Selection for Trades.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesMinor Group7210 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesNOC Code72101

common_job_titles: Mould Maker (for Plastics or Metal Casting)
duties: Design & Layout: Read and interpret complex blueprints and 3D CAD models to determine specifications for tools, dies, and fixtures.
Machining: Operate a variety of machine tools (lathes, mills, grinders) and CNC equipment to cut, shape, and finish metal to extremely tight tolerances (often within 0.001mm).
Assembly: Fit and assemble parts using hand tools and verify dimensions using high-precision measuring instruments like micrometers and CMMs (Coordinate Measuring Machines).
Testing: Heat-treat tools or dies to improve hardness and durability; test completed tools to ensure they produce parts that meet engineering standards.
Repair: Repair and modify existing dies, tools, and gauges to extend their production life or adapt them to new product designs.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 4-to-5-year tool and die making apprenticeship program OR a combination of over 5 years of work experience and industry-certified courses.

Certification (Trade Seal): * Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all provinces except Quebec.Highly recommended to obtain the Red Seal Endorsement, which allows for interprovincial mobility and is highly valued by Canadian employers.
Highly recommended to obtain the Red Seal Endorsement, which allows for interprovincial mobility and is highly valued by Canadian employers.
Proficiency in CAD/CAM software (Mastercam, SolidWorks) is a standard requirement in 2026.
salary_prospects: Note: Expert Tool and Die Makers in the aerospace or automotive sectors often earn over $110,000 annually when factoring in overtime and specialized premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. With the federal government's massive investment in the Critical Minerals Strategy and the Net-Zero Accelerator, the need for precision tooling to manufacture high-tech green components is projected to grow by 12% over the next decade. Retirement rates in this trade are high, ensuring that new immigrants will have their pick of senior roles.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Tool and Die Makers is Critical, with a severe labor shortage reported across the Canadian manufacturing belt. In 2026, Ontario (Windsor-Oshawa corridor) and Quebec are experiencing a massive surge in demand due to the re-tooling of automotive plants for EV battery and motor housing production. Because the apprenticeship takes years to complete, there is a significant "Skills Gap" that the Canadian government is actively filling through Express Entry and PNP programs.
Advanced Manufacturing Hub: Work in world-class facilities utilizing 5-axis CNC machining, EDM (Electrical Discharge Machining), and 3D metal printing.
Exceptional Wages: Tool and Die Making is one of the highest-paid skilled trades in Canada, often exceeding the wages of many white-collar professions.
Industry 4.0 Integration: Bridge the gap between traditional craftsmanship and digital manufacturing using CAD/CAM and AI-driven precision tools.
Direct PR Pathway: This role is a top priority in the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Category-Based Draws, often resulting in invitations with lower CRS scores.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary pathway for tradespeople with at least 2 years of experience.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): Ideal for those with a job offer in Ontario’s booming manufacturing sector.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: A fast-track route for workers supporting BC’s high-tech and specialized manufacturing hubs.
